source.method: Curated from Copilot session_store by mining specific repo sessions and persisting only sanitized excerpts.
source.note: This file is intentionally digested provenance, not a raw session export. It exists to show which canonical cases are grounded in real prior work and which new archetypes emerged from history.

entries.excerpt: Add automated evals and tests ... fix request/example casing drift ... surface ambiguity metadata ... before they reach LLM agents.
entries.rationale: This is the same identity-precision problem the member-disambiguation case is meant to catch: a wrapper surface can look plausible while still pointing agents at the wrong field or symbol.

entries.summary: Cache removal and engine extraction planning
entries.excerpt: Remove the call hierarchy git-SHA cache ... extract the shared call hierarchy engine into backend-shared so the IntelliJ backend can also implement CALL_HIERARCHY.
entries.rationale: This work only makes sense if caller tracing is attached to the exact function identity; that is the same failure mode the function-disambiguation case measures.

entries.excerpt: Extract source-index persistence/reference-indexing into a shared :index-store module used by both standalone and IntelliJ backends.
entries.rationale: This session was about making reference indexing real, shared, and exhaustive across hosts rather than ad hoc or backend-local.

entries.excerpt: The user wants four features implemented ... type hierarchy support on the IntelliJ plugin backend ... all changes go through the existing JSON-RPC daemon architecture.
entries.rationale: Although this was type hierarchy work rather than sealed-only traversal, it reflects the same real demand for exhaustive subtype discovery instead of text search guesses.

entries.excerpt: Blocking — alias imports break in Work Item 3 ... `ImportAnalysis.insertImportEdit()` does not preserve `as Alias` ... preserve the alias in the replacement import edit.
entries.rationale: This is a concrete history example of why safe rename must be multi-file, import-aware, and validated rather than treated like textual replacement.

entries.excerpt: The user wants four features implemented: compound "write a new X" skill scripts for LLM-driven code generation ... import-aware rename ... a new `workspace/files` endpoint.
entries.rationale: This session created the repo's write-and-validate wrapper surface in response to a real need for bounded edits that could be checked immediately instead of orphaned manual file mutations.

entries.summary: Improving Kast skill from a real failure log
entries.excerpt: Scaffold request shape (the core bug): correct is `targetFile`; wrong is `filePaths`. `workspaceRoot` also must be set explicitly unless exported.
entries.rationale: This was a real production failure in scaffold usage. The large-class scaffold case exists to ensure structural summaries are requested through the right semantic surface instead of falling back to raw file reads.

entries.excerpt: Attach `DeclarationScope` as an optional field on `Symbol`. Enable it by default on `outline` responses and make it opt-in on `resolve` and `workspace-symbol`.
entries.rationale: This session sharpened the structure-first summary surfaces that scaffold-style workflows depend on.

entries.excerpt: A new `workspace/files` JSON-RPC endpoint exposing module structure and file lists.
entries.rationale: This is the direct history source for the workspace-discovery case: a real request to expose module and file inventory as a first-class semantic operation.

entries.summary: Cache removal and engine extraction planning
entries.excerpt: Extract the shared call hierarchy engine into `backend-shared` so the IntelliJ backend can also implement `CALL_HIERARCHY`.
entries.rationale: The impact-analysis case measures the same real workflow: bounded caller tracing that survives backend boundaries and stays attached to exact call graph edges.

entries.excerpt: Extract source-index persistence/reference-indexing into a shared `:index-store` module used by both standalone and IntelliJ backends.
entries.rationale: This session is a direct cross-module flow problem: data and references had to move across module seams without losing semantic meaning.

entries.excerpt: Extract shared PSI analysis utilities into a new `backend-shared` module ... then create an IntelliJ plugin backend and parity tests.
entries.rationale: This is another concrete example of tracing and preserving behavior as symbols and analysis utilities flow across module boundaries.

novel_archetypes.title: Normalize released JSON and discriminator contracts
novel_archetypes.summary: History shows repeated pain around wrapper JSON casing, discriminator consistency, and keeping fixtures/docs aligned with the code. This is broader than any current canonical core case.

source_entries.excerpt: Normalize all naming throughout the entire system ... convert everything into camelCase, and all `type` discriminators are on field `type`, with values in CAPS_CASE.
source_entries.rationale: This is a concrete history-derived benchmark candidate around contract coherence rather than just symbol navigation.

novel_archetypes.title: Thread richer declaration payloads through semantic read surfaces
novel_archetypes.summary: History shows demand not just for symbol names, but for declaration ranges and optional body/source payloads on outline, resolve, and workspace-symbol.

source_entries.excerpt: Capture the full PSI text range and optional source text of any declaration ... enabled by default on `outline` and opt-in on `resolve` and `workspace-symbol`.
source_entries.rationale: This is a genuine history-backed task shape not isolated in the current benchmark catalog.

novel_archetypes.title: Port shell wrappers into typed Kotlin CLI commands
novel_archetypes.summary: History shows a broader task family around replacing ad hoc shell wrappers with typed Kotlin CLI surfaces, plus evaluation and CI wiring to keep them from drifting.

source_entries.excerpt: Rewrite the skill wrappers in Kotlin inside `kast-cli`, build a skill evaluation engine, and wire it into CI for regression tracking.
source_entries.rationale: This is a strong candidate for future benchmark expansion because it couples correctness, scope control, and operator ergonomics in one real task family.
